stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 3 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 8 observations with mean 54.3 % (within-trial site SD = 6.6). trial site B has 20 observations with mean 70.1 % (within-trial site SD = 6.7). trial site C has 20 observations with mean 44.7 % (within-trial site SD = 4.5). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.9 and 14.9 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site C: 44.8 % (shrunk from raw 44.7). Range [44.8, 45.0] as between-trial site SD varies from 5.9 to 14.9. Pooled mean: 56.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 56.9 % (weighted average of 3 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=9.9: B = 0.99.
Interval [44.8, 45.0] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (44.7) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (56.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
